title: hieracium murorum subsp. siliginosum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. siliginosum, commonly known as false hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Consuming the plant is said to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Hieracium murorum subsp. siliginosum is typically used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
